name: Private Lending Group Inc.
description_short: Private Lending Group Inc. is a hard money lender offering short-term and long-term real estate investment loans with 7-day closings, no tax return requirements, and flexible credit criteria.
description_long:
Private Lending Group Inc. operates as a private lender focused primarily on real estate investment financing rather than consumer mortgages. Founded and led by Daniel Wynn Arguello, the company has funded over $50,000,000 in loans throughout its operating history. While they market themselves under a mortgage category, their core business model centers on serving real estate investors, brokers, and distressed property buyers with alternative lending products.

The company offers multiple lending products tailored to real estate investors: rehab loans for property renovation projects, investment loans for purchase opportunities, rental property loans, cash-out refinance options, residential property tax loans, and commercial real estate financing. They also purchase distressed residential properties for cash and accept investor capital for mortgage and trust deed investments at up to 10% interest. Their stated value proposition emphasizes speed (7-day closings), flexibility (no minimum credit scores, no appraisals required, no tax return requirements), and approval likelihood ("We Say Yes When The Banks Say No").

What distinguishes Private Lending Group from traditional mortgage lenders is their hard money lending model, faster turnaround times, and explicit targeting of borrowers rejected by conventional banks. They operate a three-part business: direct lending to investors, broker partnerships with commissions, and investment syndication for passive income seekers. Their website features testimonials from real estate investors and brokers praising quick closings and flexibility on difficult deals. The company maintains contact channels including a 312 area code phone number, email, and social media presence.

As a private lender, Private Lending Group operates outside traditional mortgage regulatory frameworks. Investors should recognize that hard money loans typically carry higher interest rates than conventional mortgages, shorter loan terms, and are designed for short-term real estate projects rather than primary residence financing. The lack of transparency regarding specific rates, terms, and fees on their website is typical for hard money lenders who quote individually. Their business model depends on borrowers with poor credit, no documentation, or time-sensitive deals—not mainstream consumer mortgage applicants.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 7-day closing timeline, significantly faster than traditional mortgage lenders
No tax return or income documentation requirements for qualification
No minimum credit score requirements, lending to borrowers banks reject
No appraisal programs available, reducing typical closing delays
Funded over $50,000,000 historically, demonstrating operational scale and capital availability
Multiple loan types available: rehab, investment, rental, cash-out, commercial, and property tax loans
Dual revenue streams as both lender and property buyer create flexibility for distressed situations
cons: Hard money loans typically carry significantly higher interest rates than conventional mortgages (rates not disclosed on website)
Short loan terms standard in hard money lending, not suitable for long-term primary residence financing
No transparent fee schedule or rate information provided online; requires direct contact for quotes
Broker commission model and investment syndication may create conflicts of interest in deal structuring
Limited regulatory oversight compared to traditional mortgage lenders; borrower protections differ substantially
best_for: Real estate investors seeking rehab or short-term project financing with tight timelines
Borrowers with damaged credit or minimal documentation rejected by traditional lenders
Brokers needing reliable, fast funding sources for client deals and earning commission fees
Property flippers and investors buying distressed properties requiring rapid capital deployment
diagnosis: Private Lending Group is best suited for real estate investors, brokers, and property flippers needing fast capital for short-term projects, not primary residence homebuyers. The primary caveat is that hard money loans carry substantially higher costs than conventional mortgages and are designed for temporary financing of investment deals, making them unsuitable for mainstream mortgage consumers.
services: Rehab loans for property renovation projects
Investment property loans for real estate purchases
Rental property loans for income-producing real estate
Investor capital syndication (mortgages and trust deeds)
Quick online application and 24-hour approval process
Loan consulting services for real estate investors nationwide
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 150 N Michigan Ave UNIT 1500, Chicago, IL 60601
